message:
chore(node): update beads issue tracker state

Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue status — reflects closed
and newly created beads from recent development including migration
scanner optimization and security/verifier economy integration work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-17mb","title":"[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks).","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unsupported major versions and missing required features hard-fail activation; version comparisons are semantic, not lexical; negotiation decisions are logged.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/manifest_negotiation.md`, `tests/conformance/manifest_negotiation_fail_closed.rs`, `artifacts/10.13/manifest_negotiation_trace.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-17mb/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-17mb/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks).", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Implement fail-closed manifest negotiation (SemVer-aware version checks, required-feature resolution, transport cap checks)." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:52.450176581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T11:32:31.228575187Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T11:32:31.228546493Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17mb","depends_on_id":"bd-1nk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-17ylx","title":"Ignore generated RCH, beads recovery, and root scratch artifacts","description":"Problem:
The repo currently shows tool-generated artifacts as untracked, including `.rch-target-*` directories, `.beads/recovery_*` snapshots, `.beads/beads.db.bad_*`, `.beads/beads.db.rebuild_*`, and several root scratch outputs (`clippy.txt`, `patch.diff`, `unwrap_list.txt`, `test_borrow.rs`, etc.). This creates noise during multi-agent work and obscures real changes.

Evidence:
- `git status --short --ignored` shows many untracked tool artifacts in the current worktree.
- Existing ignore rules cover `.rch_tmp/` and some UBS outputs, but not `.rch-target-*` or beads recovery snapshots.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Root `.gitignore` ignores generated `.rch-target-*` directories and current root scratch outputs observed in this repo.
- `.beads/.gitignore` ignores recovery snapshots and non-canonical backup/rebuild DB artifacts produced by beads recovery workflows.
- Ignore changes are narrowly scoped so tracked contracts and source files remain visible.
- `git status --short --ignored` no longer reports the known tool-generated artifacts as untracked (they may appear as ignored with `!!`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T02:38:43.989207577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T02:40:00.393059623Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T02:40:00.393034747Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["git-hygiene","tooling","workflow"]}

 {"id":"bd-2qqu","title":"[10.14] Implement virtual transport fault harness (drop/reorder/corrupt) for remote-control protocol testing.","description":"## Why This Exists

The remote-control protocol operates over a transport layer that can experience real-world faults: packet drops, reordering, and corruption. To verify that the protocol handles these faults correctly, a virtual transport fault harness injects deterministic fault schedules into the transport layer during testing, allowing developers to systematically exercise all fault classes without requiring actual network failures. This is essential for runtime invariant #9 (deterministic verification gates: fault handling is verified under controlled, reproducible conditions) and #7 (epoch barriers must survive transport faults without split-brain). The harness is seed-driven so that any discovered failure can be reproduced exactly. Downstream consumers include the 10.15 virtual transport enforcement (bd-3u6o), the 10.15 deterministic lab runtime (bd-145n), and the conformance suite (bd-3i6c).

## What This Must Do

1. Implement `VirtualTransportFaultHarness` in `tests/harness/virtual_transport_faults.rs` that wraps a transport channel and injects faults according to a deterministic schedule.
2. Support three fault classes: (a) `Drop` -- silently discard a message, (b) `Reorder` -- delay a message and deliver it after N subsequent messages, (c) `Corrupt` -- flip specified bits in the message payload.
3. Fault schedules are deterministic and derived from a seed: `FaultSchedule::from_seed(seed: u64, config: &FaultConfig) -> FaultSchedule` produces the same sequence of faults for the same seed.
4. `FaultConfig` specifies per-class fault probability, maximum reorder depth, corruption bit count, and total fault budget (max faults per test run).
5. Every injected fault is recorded in a `FaultLog` with: `(fault_id, fault_class, message_id, timestamp, fault_details)`. The fault log is included in repro bundles (bd-2808).
6. Implement pre-built fault scenarios: (a) `no_faults` (baseline), (b) `moderate_drops` (5% drop rate), (c) `heavy_reorder` (20% reorder, depth 5), (d) `light_corruption` (1% corrupt, 1 bit), (e) `chaos` (all three at high rates).
7. Produce a spec document defining the harness architecture, fault classes, schedule derivation, and scenario catalog.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Harness supports deterministic fault schedules from seed; scenarios cover drop/reorder/corrupt classes; reproductions include exact fault sequence.
- Same seed + config produces identical fault sequence across runs (verified by comparing fault logs from two runs).
- Drop fault causes message to be absent from receiver's input.
- Reorder fault causes message to arrive after the specified number of subsequent messages.
- Corrupt fault flips exactly the specified bits in the payload.
- All pre-built scenarios execute without harness errors.
- Fault log for a 1000-message run with `chaos` scenario contains the expected number of faults (within statistical bounds of configured probabilities).
- Fault log is serializable and can be replayed to reproduce exact fault sequence.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: fault schedule determinism (same seed = same faults); drop fault message absence; reorder fault delivery order; corrupt fault bit-flip accuracy; fault config validation (reject negative probabilities, zero budget).
- Integration tests: run remote-control protocol under each pre-built scenario; verify protocol correctness under `moderate_drops` and `heavy_reorder`; verify corruption detection under `light_corruption`; full `chaos` scenario with protocol safety assertions.
- Conformance tests: `tests/harness/virtual_transport_faults.rs` -- harness self-tests and scenario execution.
- Structured logs: `FAULT_INJECTED` (fault_id, fault_class, message_id, trace_id), `FAULT_SCHEDULE_CREATED` (seed, fault_count, trace_id), `FAULT_CAMPAIGN_COMPLETE` (scenario_name, total_faults, total_messages, trace_id), `FAULT_LOG_EXPORTED` (fault_count, log_size_bytes, trace_id).

## Expected Artifacts

- `tests/harness/virtual_transport_faults.rs` -- fault harness implementation and self-tests
- `docs/testing/virtual_transport_harness.md` -- harness architecture and scenario catalog
- `artifacts/10.14/virtual_fault_campaign_results.json` -- campaign results from scenario execution
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-2qqu/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-2qqu/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

- Upstream: bd-1nfu (RemoteCap requirement -- provides the remote operation capability boundary that the harness wraps).
- Downstream: bd-3u6o (10.15 virtual transport enforcement), bd-145n (10.15 deterministic lab runtime), bd-3i6c (conformance suite), bd-2ko (10.11 product lab runtime adoption), bd-3epz (section gate), bd-5rh (section gate).","acceptance_criteria":"Harness supports deterministic fault schedules from seed; scenarios cover drop/reorder/corrupt classes; reproductions include exact fault sequence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:59.145618233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:22:15.602104118Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:22:15.602070035Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qqu","depends_on_id":"bd-1nfu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Extend .gitignore so multi-agent sessions do not keep surfacing these transient files as untracked noise.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T02:40:03.738736329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T02:41:34.236896411Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T02:41:34.236872927Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2qyqh","title":"Fix fail-closed auth threshold in admission_budget and atomic wrapping in region_ownership","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-27T03:53:04.385559653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T03:53:04.700035204Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T03:53:04.699941760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2r9uf","title":"[fresh-eyes] Remote bulkhead request identity must fail closed on empty/duplicate handles","description":"Fresh-eyes source audit found that crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs uses request_id as the stable queued-request handle in poll_queued(), but acquire() never rejects empty or duplicate request_ids. That can make queue ownership ambiguous and can issue multiple permits for the same logical remote request. Tighten the contract so request identity is explicit and fail-closed, add focused regressions, and validate with local hygiene plus rch-only heavy checks if needed.","notes":"Source reread of `crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs` still matches the intended bead contract: empty request IDs fail closed, duplicate active/queued request IDs are rejected, active request identity is released symmetrically through `outstanding_permits`, and the direct-acquire fast path now routes behind existing queue waiters to preserve FIFO admission.

Validation status refresh on 2026-03-25:
- earlier feature-correct `rch` validation for this bead stalled near sibling `frankenengine-engine`
- fresh validation on sibling bead `bd-1zabb` has now identified a concrete current blocker on the same feature-enabled compile path: rustc fails remotely at `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/runtime_diagnostics_cli.rs:5072` with `error: prefix `false` is unknown` plus `error[E0765]: unterminated double quote string`
- local follow-up inspection shows the visible local text for that engine file currently renders clean and `git diff` for that file is empty, so the blocker appears to be sibling `franken_engine` churn / remote-sync state rather than a demonstrated `remote_bulkhead` bug in `franken_node`

Interpretation: `bd-2r9uf` remains source-complete in this repo and is currently validation-blocked by the same sibling-engine compile instability affecting other `extended-surfaces` checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RubyWolf","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:13:18.334445419Z","created_by":"DarkPuma","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:40:25.574176185Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:40:25.574084654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","bulkhead","remote"],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"DarkPuma","text":"2026-03-22 DarkPuma completed the fresh-eyes remote bulkhead request-identity pass in crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs. Root cause: request_id is the only stable queued-request handle for poll_queued(), but acquire() previously accepted empty and duplicate request_ids, which made queue ownership ambiguous and could issue multiple permits for the same logical remote request. Source fix: RemoteBulkhead now fails closed on empty request IDs (RB_ERR_INVALID_REQUEST_ID), rejects duplicate request IDs across active and queued state (RB_ERR_DUPLICATE_REQUEST), tracks outstanding permits as permit_id -> request_id so release clears active request identity symmetrically, and refuses to promote a queued request if that request_id is already active. Added focused regressions for empty request IDs, duplicate active request IDs, and duplicate queued request IDs. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs. Focused heavy validation stayed rch-only: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-node request_id -- --nocapture selected worker vmi1264463, progressed through dependency compilation, reached Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0 (/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine), and then went output-silent for >60s with no verdict. Recording this bead as source-complete and infra-limited rather than remotely green.","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:19:51Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"DarkPuma","text":"2026-03-22 DarkPuma fresh-eyes follow-up on crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs found and fixed a second-order FIFO bug in the same queue-admission path. Root cause: after a release created capacity, acquire() still issued a fresh permit immediately whenever in_flight < max_in_flight, even if older queued requests already existed. That let a brand-new request leapfrog the queue head before poll_queued() had a chance to admit the older waiter, violating the module comment about deterministic queue behavior. Source fix: queue-mode admission now respects existing waiters by refusing the direct-acquire fast path whenever queue waiters already exist, queues new arrivals behind those waiters instead, and records waiters_ahead in the queue event detail. Added focused regression fresh_acquire_cannot_leapfrog_existing_queue_waiter proving queued-1 is promoted before queued-2 after capacity opens. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs. Focused heavy validation stayed rch-only: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-node fresh_acquire_cannot_leapfrog_existing_queue_waiter -- --exact --nocapture selected worker vmi1293453, progressed through dependency compilation, reached Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0 (/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine), and then went output-silent for >60s with no verdict, reproducing the shared remote stall pattern. Bead remains source-complete and infra-limited.","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:26:44Z"},{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"BoldLake","text":"2026-03-23 BoldLake re-ran focused rch-only validation for crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs with: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 RUSTFLAGS='-Cdebuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-bd-2r9uf cargo test -p frankenengine-node remote_bulkhead -- --nocapture. Worker vmi1167313 returned exit 101 after progressing into sibling dependency compilation; rustc for /data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/lib.rs was SIGKILLed before remote_bulkhead tests executed. No new source gap surfaced in remote_bulkhead.rs beyond the already-landed request-identity and FIFO fixes, so this bead remains infra-blocked rather than source-open.","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:47:41Z"},{"id":356,"issue_id":"bd-2r9uf","author":"SunnySpring","text":"2026-03-25 SunnySpring verification refresh: source reread of crates/franken-node/src/remote/remote_bulkhead.rs confirms the bead logic is already present in-tree (empty request IDs fail closed, duplicate active/queued request IDs are rejected, active request identity is tracked via outstanding_permits, and the FIFO no-leapfrog regression test is present). Fresh offloaded extended-surfaces compile evidence is now green: build 29769411858530513 on worker vmi1149989 ran  and exited 0.
